content.text: ElizaOS 1.0.0-alpha has been released. Check out the details here: https://t.co/96gie59Sud
Another announcement for ElizaOS 1.0.0-alpha is available here: https://t.co/MheHUnX5EG
A new pull request titled 'v2-fix-migrations' has been submitted. More details can be found here: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/3888
A fix has been made to update Docker files for v2. The pull request can be reviewed here: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/3887

The cryptocurrency market includes various digital assets with different price points. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is currently valued at $82,693.37, while Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) is priced at $1,921.62. Solana (SOL) is trading at $125.31, and ai16z is valued at $0.1687.

categories.title: Implementation of Clean Command in ElizaOS

content.text: The ElizaOS project has introduced a 'clean' command to its core functionality. This update includes the addition of the clean command and the reversion of minor changes.
A second iteration of the clean command, referred to as 'V2 clean command,' has also been proposed. This version likely includes improvements or refinements over the initial implementation.

categories.title: ElizaOS Updates: Permanent Knowledge Feature and Client App Issues

content.text: A new feature has been introduced to ElizaOS, allowing agents to retain permanent knowledge through an array of strings. This ensures that specific knowledge remains available at all times, rather than being retrieved only when relevant through RAG search. The update includes documentation changes and message template updates, though further updates may be needed for other clients.

A bug has been reported in the ElizaOS client app where the microphone and read-aloud features are not functioning. Users have noted that these features previously worked as expected. The issue occurs when running the client with the appropriate environment file and attempting to use the microphone or play responses aloud.

categories.title: Recent Updates and Discussions in the Community

content.text: A user inquired about the availability of multiplayer for Nether Monsters.
ElizaOS emphasized a return to its foundational principles, encouraging builders to stay focused.
A step-by-step guide for setting up a personal blockchain assistant was shared, with thanks to Ankr for the resource.
A user asked for clarification on a tweet from @pet3rpan_.
A fun idea was proposed to visualize browser history and third-party trackers as a video game, inspired by Space Invaders or Galaxiga.
Several pull requests were merged into the ElizaOS repository, including fixes for core type declarations, GUI build and API server, and a new clean command.
A new feature was introduced to add permanent knowledge capability to an agent, ensuring certain knowledge remains always available.
A fix was implemented to update Docker files for version 2.
A bug fix was applied to ensure character plugins can be loaded from remote URLs.
A reported issue highlighted a bug where line breaks were not correctly formatted in text output.
An issue was reported where the microphone and read-aloud features were not functioning in the client app.
Six contributors merged five pull requests, bringing various improvements to the project.
